pr.title: ft/ftKirby: decompile 7 Kirby special N functions
pr.author: johnwinston

summary: Decompiled several Kirby copied-neutral-B and swallow-related functions across ftKb_SpecialN, ftKb_SpecialNZd, ftKb_SpecialNNs, and ftKb_Init, plus one header signature update. The PR body claims all 7 target functions matched via an overnight script, while the loaded bot report shows 2 new 100% matches and 5 target functions as high-percentage improvements rather than 100%, so validation evidence is mixed.

key_files.role: Added ftanim include and decompiled ftKb_SpecialN_800EF35C, a Kirby hat model/matanim loading helper using costume data, ftParts filtering, HSD_DObjAddAnimAll, ftAnim_GetNextMatAnimJointInTree, and PAD_STACK(8).
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftKb_Init.c include ft/ftanim.h and ftKb_SpecialN_800EF35C implementation

key_files.role: Updated ftKb_SpecialN_800EF35C prototype from UNK_RET to void after body recovery.
Added includes for throw/thrown-Kirby/ftanim dependencies and decompiled ftKb_SpecialNDrink0_Anim, ftKb_SpecialNDrink1_Anim, and ftKb_SpecialAirNDrink_Anim.
Decompiled ftKb_PrSpecialAirNStart_Anim for copied Jigglypuff aerial Rollout startup, including motion-state transition, callback setup, animation rate, tiny horizontal velocity, and part rotation.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Copied Jigglypuff neutral B description

key_files.role: Decompiled copied Sheik loop IASA functions and copied Mewtwo neutral-B entry helper, adding a forward declaration for fn_80106DB0 and an inline change-action helper.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftKb_SkSpecialNLoop_IASA, ftKb_SkSpecialAirNLoop_IASA, ftKb_SpecialNMt_ChangeAction, ftKb_SpecialNMt_80107568 implementations
human_pr_text: copied Sheik and Mewtwo neutral B descriptions

what_changed: Replaced placeholder comments for eight listed implementations in the diff: ftKb_SpecialN_800EF35C, ftKb_SpecialNDrink0_Anim, ftKb_SpecialNDrink1_Anim, ftKb_SpecialAirNDrink_Anim, ftKb_PrSpecialAirNStart_Anim, ftKb_SkSpecialNLoop_IASA, ftKb_SkSpecialAirNLoop_IASA, and ftKb_SpecialNMt_80107568 via an inline helper.
Added required includes for recovered callsites: ft/ftanim.h in ftKb_Init.c and ftKb_SpecialN.c, plus ftCo_Throw.h and ftCo_ThrownKirby.h in ftKb_SpecialN.c.
Updated the public prototype of ftKb_SpecialN_800EF35C from UNK_RET to void in ftKb_Init.h.
Used input checks in copied Sheik IASA functions: release B transitions to end state and assigns accessory4_cb; shoulder input transitions to cancel state.
Used animation-completion checks in drink and Jigglypuff startup anim functions to transition to fall/wait/full states or adjust callbacks and animation rate.
Used PAD_STACK in ftKb_SpecialN_800EF35C, ftKb_SpecialAirNDrink_Anim, ftKb_PrSpecialAirNStart_Anim, and ftKb_SpecialNMt_80107568.

smart_moves.text: Header signature was updated alongside the recovered body for ftKb_SpecialN_800EF35C, avoiding a stale UNK_RET declaration.
The hat-loading helper used a small local struct to describe costume data entries with HSD_Joint and HSD_MatAnimJoint fields instead of leaving raw offset-only access in the excerpt.
Copied Sheik ground and air loop IASA implementations were kept structurally parallel, differing only in the motion states used for ground versus air.
The Mewtwo neutral-B entry logic was factored into a static inline ftKb_SpecialNMt_ChangeAction that the exported function calls, suggesting a narrow codegen/matching strategy within the same file.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: static inline ftKb_SpecialNMt_ChangeAction and ftKb_SpecialNMt_80107568

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a recovered function body proves a return type, update the owning header prototype in the same change rather than leaving UNK_RET.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftKb_Init.h changes ftKb_SpecialN_800EF35C from UNK_RET to void

decomp_lessons.lesson: Small temporary structs can make recovered data-table entries readable when the diff shows adjacent typed fields are known, as with Kirby costume data entries containing joint and matanim pointers.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftKb_SpecialN_800EF35C local struct with HSD_Joint* joint and HSD_MatAnimJoint* matanim

decomp_lessons.lesson: For sibling ground/air action handlers, matching source can preserve parallel control flow while swapping only the ground/air motion-state constants.

naming_conventions.name: fp
evidence_refs: raw_diff: repeated Fighter* fp locals in all implemented functions

naming_conventions.name: gobj
evidence_refs: raw_diff: all recovered functions take Fighter_GObj* gobj

naming_conventions.name: item_gobj
naming_conventions.meaning: Item_GObj* target item object in drink animations

naming_conventions.name: victim_gobj
naming_conventions.meaning: Fighter_GObj* victim object in aerial drink capture handling

naming_conventions.name: matanimjoint
naming_conventions.meaning: HSD_MatAnimJoint* iterator in hat animation loading

naming_conventions.name: ftKb_SpecialNMt_ChangeAction
naming_conventions.meaning: static inline helper introduced for copied Mewtwo neutral-B motion-state setup

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK was used narrowly in four recovered functions, with sizes 8 or 32, presumably for stack-shape matching; the PR body claims overnight 100% verification but the bot report does not confirm all of these as 100%.
Used an inline wrapper/helper for ftKb_SpecialNMt_80107568, separating the change-action body from the exported function that only calls it and pads the stack.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftKb_SpecialNMt_80107568 calls helper then PAD_STACK(8)

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used a static const motion flag expression in ftKb_PrSpecialAirNStart_Anim instead of spelling the combined bitmask inline at the callsite.
Used comma expression '(0, GET_FIGHTER(gobj))' in two drink animation functions, likely to preserve codegen shape; the loaded evidence does not include reviewer or objdiff rationale.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching ftKb_SpecialN_800EF35C for cleanup of PAD_STACK or costume_data typing.
Search ftKb_SpecialNDrink0_Anim and ftKb_SpecialNDrink1_Anim for later removal of comma-expression GET_FIGHTER patterns.
Search ftKb_SpecialNMt_ChangeAction for whether the inline helper persisted or was refactored.
Check later validation reports for PR 2343 functions to reconcile PR body 100% claim with bot report high-percentage improvements.
Search ftKb_Init.h UNK_RET removals in ftKirby for similar header-signature recovery patterns.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR metadata, merged state, changed files
human_pr_text: author summary and claimed verification
raw_diff: full diff excerpt for all changed files
notes: No review comments or reviews are loaded, so review_feedback is empty and no reviewer intent is inferred.

The PR body says all 7 functions 100% match via overnight script, but the loaded bot report says only ftKb_SkSpecialNLoop_IASA and ftKb_SkSpecialAirNLoop_IASA are new 100% matches, while other target functions are reported as 95.20%-99.98% improvements. Treat validation as mixed rather than accepting the broad 100% claim.

The title says 7 functions, but the PR body and diff list eight named placeholder replacements if ftKb_PrSpecialAirNStart_Anim is included alongside the seven summary bullets; this intake preserves both the title and observed diff evidence without resolving the count.
PAD_STACK and comma-expression tactics are visible in the diff, but the loaded evidence does not provide narrow objdiff rationale for each tactic.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Update owning header when a recovered body proves the signature
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2343 recovered ftKb_SpecialN_800EF35C as void in ftKb_Init.c and updated ftKb_Init.h from UNK_RET to void in the same PR.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep sibling ground/air IASA handlers structurally parallel
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2343's copied Sheik ground and air loop IASA handlers share the same B-release and shoulder-cancel structure while selecting the corresponding ground or air motion states.

source_update_candidates.title: Require explicit evidence when retaining comma-expression codegen steering
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2343 contains '(0, GET_FIGHTER(gobj))' in two recovered drink animation functions. This may be a narrow matching tactic similar to PAD_STACK/register-shape steering, but the loaded evidence does not explain why it is needed. Curator may consider whether comma-expression steering should be named in matching-tactics evidence requirements.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftKb_SpecialNDrink0_Anim uses Fighter* fp = (0, GET_FIGHTER(gobj))
raw_diff: ftKb_SpecialNDrink1_Anim uses Fighter* fp = (0, GET_FIGHTER(gobj))
source_update_candidates.reason: Visible tactic-like source shape is not explicitly covered by current standards except generally as codegen steering; evidence is insufficient to make it an accepted standard without curator review.

source_update_candidates.title: Record validation discrepancy for PR 2343 if maintaining a PR validation ledger
source_update_candidates.text: The PR body claims all 7 functions 100% match via overnight script, but the loaded decomp-dev bot report records 2 new 100% matches and 7 improvements in unmatched items, with several target functions below 100%.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report showing 2 new matches and high-percentage improvements
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful for downstream consumers so they do not overstate historical validation strength.

rejection_notes: Do not promote 'all 7 functions 100% matched' as an accepted validation fact from this slice because loaded bot output conflicts with the PR body.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale; no review comments or reviews are loaded.
Do not generalize PAD_STACK use in this PR into accepted style; it remains a tactic requiring narrow evidence under existing standards.
Do not rename or semantically resolve address-style functions such as fn_80106DB0, fn_80100E0C, or fn_80105978 from this evidence alone.
